data.content:
This tombstone contains **no .NET runtime frames**. The crash is from a pure native Android app (`com.example.nativeapp`) with the following stack:

- **`libc.so`** – `strlen` (null pointer dereference at `0x0`)
- **`libutils.so`** – `android::String8::set`
- **`libnativeapp.so`** – `Java_com_example_NativeLib_processData` (JNI)
- **`libart.so`** – Android ART interpreter/JNI trampoline

There are no .NET libraries (e.g., `libmonosgen-2.0.so`, `libcoreclr.so`, `libmonodroid.so`) present in the backtrace. This is a standard Android NDK/JNI crash — the `processData` JNI function passed a null string to `strlen`. There's nothing to symbolicate on the .NET side.
data.reasoningText:
Looking at this tombstone file, I can see it's from a native Android app (`com.example.nativeapp`) that crashed with a SIGSEGV (null pointer dereference). 

The backtrace contains:
1. `libc.so` - system library (strlen)
2. `libutils.so` - system library (String8::set)
3. `libnativeapp.so` - the app's native library (Java_com_example_NativeLib_processData)
4. `libart.so` - Android Runtime (ART) frames

There are **no .NET runtime frames** in this tombstone at all. There's no `libmonosgen-2.0.so`, `libcoreclr.so`, or any other .NET-related library. This is a pure native Android/JNI crash - the app is `com.example.nativeapp` using standard Android ART, not Xamarin or .NET MAUI.


Since there are no .NET runtime frames present, I won't be able to symbolicate any managed code from this crash.
